policy: Raw observations remain unchanged. Curated anchors preserve assay units, intervention identity, model system, time and applicability; missing quantities are null and are never imputed.

anchors.model: human liver tissue membrane fraction, 51 donors
anchors.applicability: Total membrane abundance conversion anchor for ABCC2/MRP2.
anchors.limitations: Does not identify the canalicular surface fraction or copies per cell; either requires matched membrane protein mass per cell and localization data.

anchors.applicability: Constrains human inflammation-response direction for BSEP total protein.
anchors.limitations: IL-6/IL-1beta increased protein despite reduced mRNA; no numeric fold is taken from the panel and total protein is not surface activity.

anchors.applicability: Constrains human inflammation-response direction for MRP2 total protein.
anchors.limitations: All three cytokines reduced MRP2 protein and mRNA; a numeric fold and surface-localized activity are not supplied.

anchors.applicability: Drug-specific CYP7A1 response target when dasatinib exposure is represented.
anchors.limitations: Not an isolated BSEP knockout or generic BSEP-loss target; the range spans tested concentrations and error/n are not available in the abstract-level panel.

anchors.applicability: Drug-specific CYP7A1 response target when pazopanib exposure is represented.
anchors.limitations: Not an isolated BSEP knockout or generic BSEP-loss target; the range spans tested concentrations and error/n are not available in the abstract-level panel.

anchors.applicability: Upper-bound validation for the matching TKI exposure protocol.
anchors.limitations: The value is 'up to', pools multiple TKIs/conditions and is extracellular; it is not an equality target for BSEP loss.

anchors.applicability: Upper-bound intracellular bile-acid validation for the matching TKI exposure protocol.
anchors.limitations: The value is 'up to' and pools multiple drug/condition results; it is not a universal BSEP-loss fold change.

anchors.model: HepaRG spheroid under repeated chlorpromazine plus bile-acid-mixture exposure
anchors.limitations: Not primary hepatocyte intracellular bile-acid kinetics and not BSEP loss alone; repeated drug plus bile-acid co-exposure is required.

anchors.model: primary human hepatocytes and differentiated HepaRG
anchors.applicability: Constrains high-GCDC human hepatocyte death toward oncotic necrosis rather than rat-like apoptosis.
anchors.limitations: Figure-level dose-response values were not transcribed; 'little/no death below 3 mM' is not promoted to a calibrated threshold.

anchors.model: pediatric Alagille explanted liver versus control
anchors.applicability: Supports chronic cholestatic disease association with hepatocyte senescence markers.
anchors.limitations: Cross-sectional genetic disease tissue, not an isolated hepatocyte time course; JAG1/NOTCH biology and bile-duct paucity confound a direct bile-acid-only interpretation.

anchors.model: primary sclerosing cholangitis liver tissue versus control
anchors.applicability: Supports pressure-associated canalicular dilation, altered connectivity and rosette formation at tissue scale.
anchors.limitations: Tissue-network phenotype is not a single-cell BSEP-loss response; figure/source data are required before numeric geometry calibration.
